frameworks.name: population coding
frameworks.one_line: subpopulations within STN carry dissociable codes for kinematics versus action switches

frameworks.name: global inhibition of movement
frameworks.one_line: the view that STN broadly suppresses ongoing actions during switches

frameworks.name: beta-band motor inhibition
frameworks.one_line: the account that elevated STN beta power drives action stopping

core_question: Does the human subthalamic nucleus globally inhibit ongoing movement when an action plan must be replaced mid-action?
key_assumptions: STN single-unit and LFP activity in Parkinson's patients reflect normal STN computation
an extended reaching task with mid-trajectory changes isolates action switching from action withholding
manipulations: ongoing reach trajectory was changed mid-action versus continued unchanged
compared switching trials to standard reaching
key_findings: STN firing decreased rather than increased during action switches
STN beta power and beta spike entrainment did not rise during switches
one STN subpopulation encoded movement kinematics and direction
a separate STN subpopulation encoded unexpected action switches
interpretations: STN does not implement a global movement-stopping signal during action replacement
STN carries an inhomogeneous population code supporting both planning and plan revision
